title: One lakh roll numbers, a million lookups

mcq.question_md: You load 1,00,000 registered roll numbers once. Then, for every entry scan at the exam hall, you must answer: "is this roll number registered?" — lakhs of times. Which structure keeps each check fastest?
options: int[] array, loop and compare each element
mcq.explanation_md: `HashSet.contains()` hashes the roll number and jumps straight to the right bucket — roughly **O(1)** per check, no matter how many entries. All three other options do a **linear scan**: they walk element by element, averaging ~50,000 comparisons per check here, which multiplied by lakhs of lookups becomes painfully slow. The tempting answer is ArrayList — it feels like the "default" collection, and `contains()` reads identically in code. But same method name, completely different cost: list `contains` is O(n), set `contains` is O(1). Choosing a structure by how it will be *queried* is exactly the instinct interviewers test.
